description:
## Goal

Fix the misleading priority comment in
`RefreshLoop::FeedFetcherConcurrent.best_available_feed`
(`src/fetcher/feed_fetcher_concurrent.cr`).

## Why

The current comment says:

    # Priority: fresh-good > stale-good > fresh-bad > stale-bad

But the actual code:

    return fetched if fetched && !fetched.failed?
    return existing if existing && !existing.failed?
    fetched || existing || FeedFetcher.instance.build_error_feed(feed, "Failed to fetch")

returns `existing_good` when both `fetched_bad` and
`existing_good` are present. The spec written in
`quickhea-tos` documents the actual behavior with a
"the actual implementation returns existing_good in this
case" comment, but the source-level comment is still wrong
and will mislead future readers.

## Fix

Update the source comment to match the actual behavior:

    # Resolve the best available data for a feed.
    # Priority: any good (fetched first, then existing) > any bad
    # (fetched first, then existing) > synthetic error.
    # In other words: prefer a known-good value; if neither is
    # good, take whatever we have; build an error only when both
    # fetched and existing are nil.

## Acceptance

- The comment in `feed_fetcher_concurrent.cr` is updated
- The implementation is unchanged
- `quickhea-tos` spec still passes (the test on line ~36
  asserts the actual behavior; it should still pass)
- Full suite still passes
- `just nix-build` succeeds
title: Fix misleading priority comment in best_available_feed
